chunks.text: Weekend Staffing for Instructional Labs Weekend Staffing for Instructional Labs Sat and Sun tasks group email addresses off-hours trouble calls when students break the rules The Sat and Sun tasks are: Once a day before 5pm: task procedure 1) Confir

Once a day before 5pm: task procedure 1) Confirm that mail server on Imail is working; browse inst@eecs email for trouble reports Read and send mail through imail.eecs.berkeley.edu . A log of mail activity is /var/log/maillog on Imail. Queued,

il activity is /var/log/maillog on Imail. Queued, undelivered mail is in /var/spool/mqueue on Imail. See imail:/etc/motd for information about restarting the spam filters, mail server and Postgres server if Imail is rebooted. 2) Walk through these

rver if Imail is rebooted. 2) Walk through these labs; note any major problems; note "no food" violations Soda (271,273,275,277,330,349) Cory (105,199) Sutardja Dai (200) For unattended login sessions, log the user out and send the user a notice us

ns, log the user out and send the user a notice using the UNIX command /share/b/adm/bin/send-logged-in-reminder.pl . For " no food " violations, report computer name, time and description of offense so we can identify the user later. You can warn t

o we can identify the user later. You can warn the user that you are doing this and give the user a chance to remove their food from the lab. If the user complies politely, you do not have to make the report. 3) Check SunRays for 'gecko' or oth

the report. 3) Check SunRays for 'gecko' or other problems Soda (271, 273, 275) Servers: nova.cs, star.cs, solar.cs Cory (199) Server: c199.eecs Make a note of any Sunrays that have an hourglass or other problem. See ~inst/docs/sunrays.help

s or other problem. See ~inst/docs/sunrays.help for information about the keystrokes that clear some SunRay problems. (Admins who can login as 'root' on the Sunray server can run /share/b/adm/bin/fixblank or /share/b/adm/bin/fixgecko on appropri

/fixblank or /share/b/adm/bin/fixgecko on appropriate SunRay server to clear problems.) 4) Re-stock the printers with paper if needed. Confirm that printers are running their queues. Restock paper in the printers if needed. Printer paper is

er in the printers if needed. Printer paper is stored in 130A Cory and 253 Soda. Windows computers use the print spooler scotland.eecs . Windows computers use the print spooler iprint.eecs . UNIX printer commands: see /share/b/pub/printers.help Pr

rinter commands: see /share/b/pub/printers.help Print spooler administration on iprint.eecs (Nov 2007): LPRng logs: Accounting logs: LPRng configuration file: To kill and restart lprng: /usr/local/var/spool/[building]/[printer] /usr/local/var/log /us

/spool/[building]/[printer] /usr/local/var/log /usr/local/etc/printcap /etc/init.d/lprng [stop|start] Print spooler administration on scotland.eecs (Apr 2010): Scotland is a Windows server using lprng on Cygwin. So work in a Cygwin bash shell to m

on Cygwin. So work in a Cygwin bash shell to manage the lprng files. For more information, see \\fileservice.eecs.berkeley.edu\software\ScannersCamerasPrinters\lprng\readme.txt' . 5) Confirm that the Instructional WEB server is working Go to U

at the Instructional WEB server is working Go to URL http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu and https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu Log of WEB server activity is in /share/weblogs/ on Cory. 6) Inform faculty and students if their lab is down (more than about 30

students if their lab is down (more than about 30 min) Post signs in labs, post to class newgroups and/or send email to affected users. 7) Send a brief report to "inst@eecs.berkeley.edu" List which labs you checked and any problems that you saw. O

labs you checked and any problems that you saw. Off-hours trouble calls: (for staff) campus network & computers: 642-4920 (IS&T Trouble Desk) EECS network & computers: 642-7777 (EECS help desk) Inst computers & labs: 232-3876 (Kevin)(
Inst computers & labs: 232-3876 (Kevin)(8am-10pm only please) building safety & security: 642-1032 (Facilities Management) personal safety & security: 642-6760 (UC Police) EMERGENCY: 9-91

lice) EMERGENCY: 9-911 When students break the rules: (for staff) If you see anyone eating or etc, you can politely say something like "Hi, I'm lab support staff, and my boss has told me to report anyone who

and my boss has told me to report anyone who is eating in the labs. He makes people clean the labs as a punishment. But if you remove the food from the lab now, I don't have to report it. Thanks." You can report them to Kevin by i

it. Thanks." You can report them to Kevin by identifying the time and the computer they were logged into. This may not always work (such as if they are using a laptop or working in a group), but it is good enough. It is better to let t

, but it is good enough. It is better to let the student get away with it than to falsely accuse or argue with the student. Group email addresses: inst@eecs Supports all Instructional servers and these labs: 271, 273, 274, 285 277, 330, 34

s and these labs: 271, 273, 274, 285 277, 330, 349 Soda 105, 119, 199 Cory C30, C50 Hearst Field Annex esg(at)eecs Supports the EE and CS150 labs that include electronics and instrumentation (125, 140, 218, 204 and 353 Cory) and labs tha

on (125, 140, 218, 204 and 353 Cory) and labs that are assigned to student groups (246 Cory, 444 Soda). See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/iesglabs.html for more info. printers@cory.eecs an alias for staff (not students), since some of th

n alias for staff (not students), since some of the support work is done by people who are not in the "inst@eecs" group. It goes to inst@eecs as well as Ming Wong and Ferenc Kovac. "inst@eecs" does the software/spooler maintenance. Ming doe

does the software/spooler maintenance. Ming does printer hardware repairs. ISG and ESG each replace the paper and toners in their own labs from the shared supplies in 130A Cory and 251 Soda. Students are told to send bug reports about the prin

udents are told to send bug reports about the printers to their lab support group - either "inst@eecs" or "esg@eecs". This should be posted on each printer. csav@cs, eeav@eecs Supports the projectors and audio in classrooms and auditoriums in

jectors and audio in classrooms and auditoriums in Soda and Cory Halls. Includes some ESG staff as well as Chris Colbert (Cory Hall staff), CS office staff and some faculty. Thursday, May 02, 2019 kevinm@eecs.berkeley.edu
9 kevinm@eecs.berkeley.edu
